title: Syntherisma humifusa
intro: Syntherisma humifusa, also known as Sweetroot or Sweetroot Broomrape, is a perennial parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of surrounding plants, often using the roots of grasses and other low-growing vegetation as a host. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Syntherisma humifusa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Sweetroot is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is also used in some herbal remedies in combination with other plants to support respiratory health and immune function.
